references.text:
[RuSz78] Ruzsa, I. Z. and Szemer\'{e}di, E., Triple systems with no six points carrying three triangles. Combinatorics (Proc. Fifth Hungarian Colloq.,
Keszthely, 1976), Vol. II (1978), 939-945.

remarks.text:
Ruzsa and Szemer\'{e}di \cite{RuSz78} proved that $e(n,r)=o(n^2)$ for any fixed $r$.

See also [80].

statement.text: Let $e(n,r)$ be minimal such that every graph on $n$ vertices with at least $e(n,r)$ edges, each edge contained in at least one triangle, must have an edge contained in at least $r$ triangles. Let $r\geq 2$. Is it true that\[e(n,r+1)-e(n,r)\to \infty\]as $n\to \infty$? Is it true that\[\frac{e(n,r+1)}{e(n,r)}\to 1\]as $n\to \infty$?
